title: T-Rex: Tactile-Reactive Dexterous Manipulation
summary: T-Rex pushes the frontier of tactile-reactive dexterous manipulation, addressing why current Vision-Language-Action (VLA) models largely overlook touch or encode it only with static cues. It contributes a large-scale 100-hour tactile-rich dataset collected via a data-efficient recipe centered on elementary motor primitives, plus a variable-rate Mixture-of-Transformers (MoT) architecture with a temporal tactile VQ-VAE encoder that exploits high-frequency touch signals without sacrificing existing VLA capabilities. Across 12 manipulation tasks requiring delicate force control and deformable-object handling, the tactile-reactive policies achieve over 30% higher average success rate than the strongest baseline.
